proof_sketch: The removed __syncthreads() is after mean calculation and before variance computation. All threads read shared_mem[0] to compute mean before entering the variance loop. The only subsequent write to shared_mem[0] is from thread 0 after its variance loop. Since all threads read shared_mem[0] before any write occurs (the read precedes the loop that contains the write), there is no data race. Instruction ordering (load before store) ensures correctness without the barrier.

Since all threads read shared_mem[0] before any write occurs (the read precedes the loop that contains the write), there is no data race. Instruction ordering (load before store) ensures correctness without the barrier.",
  "survival_reason": "The removed synchronization barrier is redundant because the shared memory location shared_mem[0] is read by all threads before any thread writes to it. Each thread computes mean by reading shared_mem[0] before starting its variance reduction loop. The only write to shared_mem[0] occurs after thread 0 completes its variance loop, which is after all threads have already read it. Thus, the barrier provides no additional ordering guarantee and its removal does not affect the computation result. All tested inputs, including extreme magnitudes, subnormals, and adversarial distributions, produce bitwise identical outputs.",
